rules.standard: Lawful processing — transparency / privacy notice (NDPA s.27)
rules.intent: A data subject must be given a privacy notice describing what personal data is collected, the legal basis, and their rights.
rules.fix: Publish a privacy notice/policy page and link it from sign-up, checkout, and the footer.

rules.standard: Consent must be freely given, specific and opt-in (NDPA s.26)
rules.intent: Non-essential cookies/trackers and secondary processing need explicit opt-in consent — pre-ticked or implied consent does not meet the bar.
rules.fix: Add a consent banner/manager whose non-essential categories default to OFF until the user opts in.

rules.intent: Consent must be collected per processing purpose (e.g. analytics, marketing) rather than as one blanket toggle.
rules.fix: Split consent into granular categories (analytics, marketing/advertising, functional) the user can accept or reject independently.

rules.standard: Data subject rights — access, portability, erasure (NDPA s.34-37)
rules.intent: Data subjects must be able to access/export their data and request deletion (right to erasure).
rules.fix: Expose export-data, data-access-request, and delete-account flows wired to a fulfilment workflow.

rules.standard: Security of processing — appropriate technical measures (NDPA s.39)
rules.intent: Personal data must be protected with appropriate technical measures such as encryption of sensitive fields at rest.
rules.fix: Encrypt sensitive personal data (PII, contact details) at rest and hash credentials.

rules.intent: Personal data must not be kept longer than necessary — a retention/soft-delete + scheduled purge lifecycle should exist.
rules.fix: Implement soft-delete with a scheduled hard-purge after the retention window, and document the retention period.

rules.standard: Applicable law — Nigeria Data Protection Act 2023, not only foreign regimes
rules.intent: A platform processing Nigerian data subjects must apply the NDPA 2023 / NDPC framework — a GDPR-only privacy posture is not sufficient.
rules.fix: Reference the NDPA 2023 / NDPC explicitly in the privacy notice and compliance docs, alongside any GDPR text.

rules.standard: Data Protection Officer — required for a Data Controller of Major Importance (NDPA s.32)
rules.intent: A Data Controller of Major Importance must designate a Data Protection Officer and publish a contact route for data-protection queries.
rules.fix: Designate a DPO and publish a data-protection contact (e.g. privacy@/dpo@) in the privacy notice.

rules.standard: Personal data breach notification within 72 hours (NDPA s.40)
rules.intent: There must be a documented breach-response plan that notifies the NDPC within 72 hours and affected data subjects without undue delay.
rules.fix: Add a breach-response runbook documenting the 72-hour NDPC notification and data-subject notification steps.

rules.standard: Cross-border transfer safeguards & processor agreements (NDPA s.41-43)
rules.intent: Personal data sent to processors abroad (payments, email, analytics) needs an adequacy basis or contractual safeguards (a DPA per processor).
rules.fix: Document cross-border transfer bases and sign a Data Processing Agreement with each sub-processor; list sub-processors in the privacy notice.
